DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ERVIN JENKINS, VANESSA CAMACHO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT MARUTI SHARANAM, LLC DBA WESTERN HOTEL. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT DIRECTS DEFENDANT TO SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS (SET ONE) NOS. 1 - 6 WITHOUT OBJECTIONS WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFFS' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE CONDITIONALLY GRANTED. DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL OF RECORD ARE ORDERED TO JOINTLY PAY PLAINTIFFS $2,172.50 IN MONETARY SANCTIONS. THEY WILL BE RELIEVED OF THE OBLIGATION TO PAY SUCH SANCTIONS IF DEFENDANT TIMELY SERVES FURTHER RESPONSES IN COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM PVESTAL@NVLAWLLP.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER VESTAL, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Maruti Sharanam, Llc Dba Western Hotel
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Betzy Bras-Gonzalez, Esq. of BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C. (415) 835-6777 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ERVIN JENKINS, VANESSA CAMACHO's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Maruti Sharanam, Llc Dba Western Hotel.

The motion is GRANTED.

A responding party may serve objections to particular requests for admission in lieu of admitting or denying them. (CCP 2033.210(b).) The objecting party has the burden of justifying the objection in the event the propounding party brings a motion to compel further responses. (Cal Judges Benchbook: Civ Proceedings-Discovery (CJER 2019), 22:30; see Coy v. Superior Court of Contra Costa County (1962) 58 Cal. 2d 210, 220 (interrogatories).) It is not ground for objection that the request is "ambiguous" unless it is so ambiguous that the responding party cannot in good faith frame an intelligent reply. (See Cembrook v. Sup.Ct. (Sterling Drug, Inc.) (1961) 56 Cal. 2d at 428-429.)

The declarations of Indrasinh Solanki and Evgenia Jansen on behalf of Defendant Maruti Sharanam, LLC dba Western Hotel ("Defendant") reveal a misapprehension as to whether the absence of a discount policy renders the requests at issue ambiguous. It does not. Plaintiffs used terms to formulate the subject requests that have a readily applicable commonsense understanding. Defendant may deny the requests to the extent it believes the requests state propositions which are inapposite to its own business practices, and it will have an opportunity to elaborate on the basis for its responses in presenting its defense and/or in the course of discovery (e.g., answering Form Interrogatory No. 17.1).

The court directs Defendant to serve verified code-compliant further responses to Plaintiffs' Requests for Admissions (Set One) Nos. 1 - 6 without objections within fifteen (15) days of the date of this Order.

Plaintiffs' requests for sanctions are conditionally GRANTED. Defendant and its counsel of record are ordered to jointly pay Plaintiffs $2,172.50 in monetary sanctions. They will be relieved of the obligation to pay such sanctions if Defendant timely serves further responses in compliance with this Order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem pvestal@nvlawllp.com

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
